sn2.26:1.2: As he was standing to one side, Rohitassa the deva’s son said to the Blessed One:
sn2.26:1.3: “Is it possible, lord, by traveling, to know or see or reach a far end of the cosmos where one doesn’t take birth, age, die, pass away or reappear?”
sn2.26:1.4: “I tell you, friend, that it isn’t possible by traveling to know or see or reach a far end of the cosmos where one doesn’t take birth, age, die, pass away, or reappear.”
sn2.26:2.2: how well that has been said by the Lord Blessed One:
sn2.26:2.3: ‘I tell you, friend, that it isn’t possible by traveling to know or see or reach a far end of the cosmos where one doesn’t take birth, age, die, pass away, or reappear.’
sn2.26:3.1: Once I was a seer named Rohitassa, a student of Bhoja, a powerful sky-walker.
sn2.26:3.2: My speed was as fast as that of a strong archer—well trained, a practiced hand, a practiced sharpshooter—shooting a light arrow across the shadow of a palm tree.
sn2.26:3.3: My stride stretched as far as the east sea is from the west.
sn2.26:3.4: To me, there came the desire:
sn2.26:3.5: ‘I will go traveling to the end of the cosmos.’
sn2.26:3.6: I—endowed with such speed, such a stride, with a one-hundred year life, a one-hundred year span—spent one hundred years traveling—apart from the time spent on eating, drinking, chewing & tasting, urinating & defecating, and sleeping to fight off weariness—but without reaching the end of the cosmos I died along the way.
sn2.26:4.1: So it’s amazing, lord, and astounding,
sn2.26:4.2: how well that has been said by the Lord Blessed One:
sn2.26:4.3: ‘I tell you, friend, that it isn’t possible by traveling to know or see or reach a far end of the cosmos where one doesn’t take birth, age, die, pass away, or reappear.’”
sn2.26:5.1: [When this was said, the Blessed One responded:] “I tell you, friend, that it isn’t possible by traveling to know or see or reach a far end of the cosmos where one doesn’t take birth, age, die, pass away, or reappear.
sn2.26:5.2: But at the same time, I tell you that there is no making an end of suffering & stress without reaching the end of the cosmos. Yet it is just within this fathom-long body, with its perception & intellect, that I declare that there is the cosmos, the origination of the cosmos, the cessation of the cosmos, and the path of practice leading to the cessation of the cosmos.”
sn2.26:6.1: It’s not to be reached by traveling,
sn2.26:6.3: And it’s not without reaching the end of the cosmos
sn2.26:6.4: that there is release from suffering & stress.
sn2.26:7.1: So, truly, an expert with regard to the cosmos,
sn2.26:7.2: intelligent, a knower of the end of the cosmos, having fulfilled the holy life,
sn2.26:7.4: concentrated, doesn’t long for this cosmos or any other.
